summary: Successfully defined and validated canonical schemas for all five relationship intelligence data products with comprehensive validation system, migration plan, and backward compatibility strategy
learnings: JSON schemas with comprehensive enum validation and nested object requirements provide robust data quality enforcement
Provenance field naming varies by schema type (processor_version vs extractor_version vs detector_version) requiring flexible validation helpers
Schema validation with business rule enforcement (tier assignment, score consistency) prevents data quality issues at ingestion time
Database migration strategy with additive-only changes and foreign key references maintains full backward compatibility
Representative sample payloads that pass validation are crucial for integration testing and preventing downstream failures
Validation helper utilities with auto-fix capabilities and batch processing significantly reduce integration friction for consumers
